```{r integration_interaction_plot}
ht_deconv = Heatmap(
  mat_deconv_Estimate,
  name = "Estimate",
  col = col_fun,
  column_title = "Deconvolution",
  column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold"),
  row_title = "Early Integration",
  row_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold"),
  cell_fun = cell_fun_maker(mat_deconv_pvalue),
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_columns = FALSE,
  show_heatmap_legend = TRUE,
  na_col = "grey90",
  border = TRUE,
  rect_gp = gpar(col = "white", lwd = 2)
)


ht_list = ht_PP_rna + ht_PP_met + ht_PP_scrna + ht_FS_rna + ht_FS_met + ht_FS_scrna + ht_deconv

draw(ht_list, 
     column_title = "Blocks interactions with early integration compare to baseline",
     column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 16, fontface = "bold"),
     heatmap_legend_side = "right",
     annotation_legend_side = "right")

# decorate_heatmap_body("Estimate", {
#   grid.text("* p<0.05  ** p<0.01  *** p<0.001", 
#             x = unit(0.5, "npc"), 
#             y = unit(-0.05, "npc"), 
#             just = "top",
#             gp = gpar(fontsize = 9, fontface = "italic"))
# })
```


# Elise's paper plot

## barplot

### Late_integration

```{r barplot_median_aggr_score_of_deconv_by_dataset}

data = results_li_all_dataset
x_var = "late_integration"
score = "score_aggreg"
module_palette = module_pal
level_palette = level_colors

  
  if (x_var != "early_integration" && x_var != "late_integration") {
    data = data[data$preprocessing_mixRNA != "nopp",]
    data = data[data$feature_selection_mixRNA != "nofs",]
    data = data[data$preprocessing_RNA != "nopp",]
    data = data[data$feature_selection_RNA != "nofs",]
    data = data[data$preprocessing_scRNA != "nopp",]
    data = data[data$feature_selection_scRNA != "nofs",]
    data = data[data$preprocessing_mixMET != "nopp",]
    data = data[data$feature_selection_mixMET != "nofs",]
    data = data[data$preprocessing_MET != "nopp",]
    data = data[data$feature_selection_MET != "nofs",]
  }
  
  search_module = which(sapply(names(module_palette), \(module_i){grepl(module_i, x = x_var)}) == 1) 
  color_module = module_pal[search_module]
  
  
  order_level_x_var = sapply(levels(data[,x_var]), \(level_i){
    median(data[data[,x_var] == level_i, score], na.rm = TRUE)
  })
  # if we look at mae, we want to look at the minimum, otherwise (score aggreg and cor) the max
  order_level_x_var = order_level_x_var[order(order_level_x_var, decreasing = !(score == "mae"))]
  data[,x_var] = factor(data[,x_var], levels = names(order_level_x_var))
  
  
  
  data %>% 
    mutate(
      dataset = recode(dataset, "invitro1" = "VITR"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "invivo1" = "VIVO"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicopseudobulk1" = "SBN5"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicodirichletNoDep1" = "SDN5"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicodirichletNoDep4CTsource1" = "SDN4"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicodirichletNoDep6CTsource1" = "SDN6"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicodirichletEMFA1" = "SDE5"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicodirichletEMFAImmuneLowProp1" = "SDEL"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicodirichletCopule1" = "SDC5"),
      dataset = factor(dataset, 
                       levels = c("VITR", "VIVO", "SBN5",
                                  "SDN5", "SDN4", "SDN6",
                                  "SDE5", "SDEL", "SDC5"))
    ) %>% 
    group_by(dataset, late_integration) %>%
    filter(late_integration != "OnlyMet" & late_integration != "OnlyRna") %>% 
    summarise(median_score_aggreg = median(score_aggreg, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
    filter(!is.na(median_score_aggreg)) %>% 
    ggplot() +
    geom_bar(aes(x = dataset, y = median_score_aggreg, fill = late_integration), stat='identity', position='dodge') +
    labs(
      # title = paste("Median ordered aggregated score for", x_var, "within early integration strategies"),
      # subtitle = "ordered by median of aggregated score",
      x = element_blank(),
      y = "Median aggregated score",
    ) +
    ylim(0, 1) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      name = "Late integration",
      values = rep(level_palette[levels(data[,x_var])], length(unique(data$dataset)))
    ) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
      panel.border = element_rect(color = color_module, fill = NA, linewidth = 3),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5)#,
      # legend.position = "bottom"
    )
  
  
  
  
  # OnlyMet and OnlyRna
  data %>% 
    mutate(
      dataset = recode(dataset, "invitro1" = "VITR"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "invivo1" = "VIVO"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicopseudobulk1" = "SBN5"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicodirichletNoDep1" = "SDN5"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicodirichletNoDep4CTsource1" = "SDN4"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicodirichletNoDep6CTsource1" = "SDN6"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicodirichletEMFA1" = "SDE5"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicodirichletEMFAImmuneLowProp1" = "SDEL"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicodirichletCopule1" = "SDC5"),
      dataset = factor(dataset, 
                       levels = c("VITR", "VIVO", "SBN5",
                                  "SDN5", "SDN4", "SDN6",
                                  "SDE5", "SDEL", "SDC5"))
    ) %>% 
    group_by(dataset, late_integration) %>%
    filter(late_integration == "OnlyMet" | late_integration == "OnlyRna") %>% 
    summarise(median_score_aggreg = median(score_aggreg, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
    filter(!is.na(median_score_aggreg)) %>% 
    ggplot() +
    geom_bar(aes(x = dataset, y = median_score_aggreg, fill = late_integration), stat='identity', position='dodge') +
    labs(
      # title = paste("Median ordered aggregated score for", x_var, "within early integration strategies"),
      # subtitle = "ordered by median of aggregated score",
      x = element_blank(),
      y = "Median aggregated score",
    ) +
    ylim(0, 1) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      name = "Uni-omic",
      values = c("grey40", "grey60")
    ) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
      panel.border = element_rect(color = color_module, fill = NA, linewidth = 3),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5)#,
      # legend.position = "bottom"
    )

```

### Early_integration

```{r barplot_median_aggr_score_of_deconv_by_dataset}

data = results_ei_all_dataset
x_var = "early_integration"
score = "score_aggreg"
module_palette = module_pal
level_palette = level_colors

  
  if (x_var != "early_integration" && x_var != "late_integration") {
    data = data[data$preprocessing_mixRNA != "nopp",]
    data = data[data$feature_selection_mixRNA != "nofs",]
    data = data[data$preprocessing_RNA != "nopp",]
    data = data[data$feature_selection_RNA != "nofs",]
    data = data[data$preprocessing_scRNA != "nopp",]
    data = data[data$feature_selection_scRNA != "nofs",]
    data = data[data$preprocessing_mixMET != "nopp",]
    data = data[data$feature_selection_mixMET != "nofs",]
    data = data[data$preprocessing_MET != "nopp",]
    data = data[data$feature_selection_MET != "nofs",]
  }
  
  search_module = which(sapply(names(module_palette), \(module_i){grepl(module_i, x = x_var)}) == 1) 
  color_module = module_pal[search_module]
  
  
  order_level_x_var = sapply(levels(data[,x_var]), \(level_i){
    median(data[data[,x_var] == level_i, score], na.rm = TRUE)
  })
  # if we look at mae, we want to look at the minimum, otherwise (score aggreg and cor) the max
  order_level_x_var = order_level_x_var[order(order_level_x_var, decreasing = !(score == "mae"))]
  data[,x_var] = factor(data[,x_var], levels = names(order_level_x_var))
  
  
  
  data %>% 
    mutate(
      dataset = recode(dataset, "invitro1" = "VITR"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "invivo1" = "VIVO"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicopseudobulk1" = "SBN5"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicodirichletNoDep1" = "SDN5"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicodirichletNoDep4CTsource1" = "SDN4"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicodirichletNoDep6CTsource1" = "SDN6"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicodirichletEMFA1" = "SDE5"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicodirichletEMFAImmuneLowProp1" = "SDEL"),
      dataset = recode(dataset, "insilicodirichletCopule1" = "SDC5"),
      dataset = factor(dataset, 
                       levels = c("VITR", "VIVO", "SBN5",
                                  "SDN5", "SDN4", "SDN6",
                                  "SDE5", "SDEL", "SDC5"))
      
    ) %>% 
    group_by(dataset, early_integration) %>%
    summarise(median_score_aggreg = median(score_aggreg, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
    ggplot() +
    geom_bar(aes(x = dataset, y = median_score_aggreg, fill = early_integration), stat='identity', position='dodge') +
    labs(
      # title = paste("Median ordered aggregated score for", x_var, "within early integration strategies"),
      # subtitle = "ordered by median of aggregated score",
      x = element_blank(),
      y = "Median aggregated score",
    ) +
    ylim(0, 1) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      name = "Early integration",
      values = rep(level_palette[levels(data[,x_var])], length(unique(data$dataset)))
    ) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
      panel.border = element_rect(color = color_module, fill = NA, linewidth = 3),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5)#,
      # legend.position = "bottom"
    )

```

## spider plot

### Late_integration

```{r spider_plot}
colors_dataset = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(name="Dark2", n=8)

score_data = results_li_all_dataset %>% 
  mutate(
    dataset = factor(dataset, 
                     levels = c("invitro1", "invivo1", "insilicopseudobulk1",
                                "insilicodirichletNoDep1", "insilicodirichletNoDep4CTsource1", "insilicodirichletNoDep6CTsource1",
                                "insilicodirichletEMFA1", "insilicodirichletEMFAImmuneLowProp1", "insilicodirichletCopule1"))
  ) %>% 
  select(c(dataset, late_integration) | ends_with("norm")) %>% 
  filter(dataset != "invivo1") %>% 
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = aid_norm:spearman_tot_norm,
    names_to = "metric_norm",
    values_to = "score"
  ) %>% 
  group_by(dataset, late_integration, metric_norm) %>%
  summarise(max_score = max(score, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = metric_norm,
    values_from = max_score
  )


levels(score_data$late_integration)
late_inte_method = "OnlyMet"


df_radarplot = as.data.frame(base::rbind(
  # rmse,       mae,       aitchison,        pearson_tot, pearson_col, pearson_row, spearman_tot, spearman_col, spearman_row
  rep(1, ncol(score_data)-2), # the max/best value for each var
  rep(0, ncol(score_data)-2), # the min/worst value for each var
  score_data[score_data$late_integration == late_inte_method,-c(1,2)]
))
# -Inf appear by max when only NA (for JSD) 
df_radarplot[df_radarplot < -10] = NA

# remove "_norm" to gain space
colnames(df_radarplot) <- substr(colnames(df_radarplot), 1, nchar(colnames(df_radarplot)) - 5)

fmsb::radarchart(
  df_radarplot, 
  axistype = 0, seg = 5,
  
  title = paste(late_inte_method),
  #custom polygon
  pcol=colors_dataset, plwd=2, plty = 1,
 
  #custom the grid
  cglcol="grey", cglty=1, axislabcol="grey50", cglwd=0.8,
 
  #custom labels
  vlcex=1, vlabels = rep("", ncol(df_radarplot))
)

# by claude to change justification of labels
n <- ncol(df_radarplot)
angles <- (pi/2) + 2 * pi * (seq_len(n) - 1) / n  # angle de chaque axe

r_label <- 1.15  # rayon pour positionner les labels (>1 = hors du graphe)
x_pos <- (r_label+0.3) * cos(angles)
y_pos <- r_label * sin(angles)

text(
  x = x_pos,
  y = y_pos,
  labels = colnames(df_radarplot),
  cex = 0.9,
  adj = c(0.5, 0.5),   # centrage horizontal / vertical
  srt = 0,             # rotation en degrés (ex: 45 pour incliné)
  font = 2,             # gras
  col = c("red", "blue", "blue", 
          "blue", "violet", "tan2", 
          "violet", "blue", "red", 
          "violet", "tan2", "violet")
)


legend(x=0.1, y=1.1,
       legend = unique(score_data$dataset),
       bty = "n", pch=20 , col=colors_dataset, text.col = "black",
       cex=1, pt.cex=1, x.intersp=.8, y.intersp=.8
)

```

### Early_integration

```{r spider_plot}
colors_dataset = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(name="Dark2", n=8)

score_data = results_ei_all_dataset %>% 
  mutate(
    dataset = factor(dataset, 
                     levels = c("invitro1", "invivo1", "insilicopseudobulk1",
                                "insilicodirichletNoDep1", "insilicodirichletNoDep4CTsource1", "insilicodirichletNoDep6CTsource1",
                                "insilicodirichletEMFA1", "insilicodirichletEMFAImmuneLowProp1", "insilicodirichletCopule1"))
  ) %>% 
  select(c(dataset, early_integration) | ends_with("norm")) %>% 
  filter(dataset != "invivo1") %>% 
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = aid_norm:spearman_tot_norm,
    names_to = "metric_norm",
    values_to = "score"
  ) %>% 
  group_by(dataset, early_integration, metric_norm) %>%
  summarise(max_score = max(score, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = metric_norm,
    values_from = max_score
  )


levels(score_data$early_integration)
early_inte_method = "OT"


df_radarplot = as.data.frame(base::rbind(
  # rmse,       mae,       aitchison,        pearson_tot, pearson_col, pearson_row, spearman_tot, spearman_col, spearman_row
  rep(1, ncol(score_data)-2), # the max/best value for each var
  rep(0, ncol(score_data)-2), # the min/worst value for each var
  score_data[score_data$early_integration == early_inte_method,-c(1,2)]
))
# -Inf appear by max when only NA (for JSD) 
df_radarplot[df_radarplot < -10] = NA

# remove "_norm" to gain space
colnames(df_radarplot) <- substr(colnames(df_radarplot), 1, nchar(colnames(df_radarplot)) - 5)

fmsb::radarchart(
  df_radarplot, 
  axistype = 0, seg = 5,
  
  title = paste(early_inte_method),
  #custom polygon
  pcol=colors_dataset, plwd=2, plty = 1,
 
  #custom the grid
  cglcol="grey", cglty=1, axislabcol="grey50", cglwd=0.8,
 
  #custom labels
  vlcex=1, vlabels = rep("", ncol(df_radarplot))
)

# by claude to change justification of labels
n <- ncol(df_radarplot)
angles <- (pi/2) + 2 * pi * (seq_len(n) - 1) / n  # angle de chaque axe

r_label <- 1.15  # rayon pour positionner les labels (>1 = hors du graphe)
x_pos <- (r_label+0.3) * cos(angles)
y_pos <- r_label * sin(angles)

text(
  x = x_pos,
  y = y_pos,
  labels = colnames(df_radarplot),
  cex = 0.9,
  adj = c(0.5, 0.5),   # centrage horizontal / vertical
  srt = 0,             # rotation en degrés (ex: 45 pour incliné)
  font = 2,             # gras
  col = c("red", "blue", "blue", 
          "blue", "violet", "tan2", 
          "violet", "blue", "red", 
          "violet", "tan2", "violet")
)




legend(x=1.4, y=1.5,
       legend =c("VITR", "VIVO", "SBN5",
                 "SDN5", "SDN4", "SDN6",
                 "SDE5", "SDEL", "SDC5"),
       bty = "n", pch=20 , col=colors_dataset, text.col = "black",
       cex=1, pt.cex=1, x.intersp=.8, y.intersp=.8, bg = "white"
)



```



# Correlations of the different metrics

## Late_integration

```{r cor_dens_plot, fig.height=9}

# Fonction personnalisée pour corrélations avec couleurs et légende
cor_fun_colored = function(data, mapping, method = "pearson", ndp = 2, sz = 4, ...) {
  x = eval_data_col(data, mapping$x)
  y = eval_data_col(data, mapping$y)
  corr = cor(x, y, method = method, use = "complete.obs")
  
  # Palette de couleurs continue de -1 à 1
  # Utilisation d'une palette divergente : bleu (négatif) - blanc (0) - rouge (positif)
  color_palette = colorRampPalette(c("#2166AC", "#F7F7F7", "#B2182B"))(100)
  color_index = round((corr + 1) * 50) + 1  # Convertit [-1,1] en [1,100]
  color_index = pmax(1, pmin(100, color_index))  # Assure que l'index est dans [1,100]
  col_cor = color_palette[color_index]
  
  ggally_text(label = paste0("Corr ", method, " :\n", round(corr, ndp)), 
              color = col_cor, 
              size = sz,
              fontface = "bold") +
    theme_void()
}

density_fun_limited = function(data, mapping, ...) {
  ggplot(data = data, mapping = mapping) +
    geom_density2d(alpha = 0.6, color = "steelblue", ...) +
    xlim(0, 1) +
    ylim(0, 1)
}

# Version finale optimisée
ggpairs_final = ggpairs(
  results_li,  # Dataset complet
  columns = c("score_aggreg", "rmse_norm", "pearson_col_norm", "spearman_col_norm", "pearson_row_norm", "spearman_row_norm", "aitchison_norm"),
  upper = list(continuous = cor_fun_colored),
  lower = list(continuous = density_fun_limited),
  # lower = list(continuous = wrap("density", alpha = 0.6, bins = 10, fill = "steelblue", xlim = c(0,1), ylim = c(0,1))),
  diag = list(continuous = wrap("densityDiag", alpha = 0.8, fill = "lightblue"))
) + 
theme(
  strip.text = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
  axis.text = element_text(size = 8)
)

print(ggpairs_final)
```

- Corrélations élevées
- distribution marginales proches

```{r cor_plot, fig.height=9}
# results_li[results_li=="NaN"] = NA
row_rm = apply(results_li,1,function(x) any(is.na(x)))
results_li_rm = results_li[!row_rm,-(1:(length(all_functions_li)+2))]

score_aggreg_order = grep("score_aggreg", colnames(results_li_rm))
normalised_metrics = grep("norm", colnames(results_li_rm))
unnormalised_metrics = (1:ncol(results_li_rm))[-c(score_aggreg_order, normalised_metrics)]
corr_metric = c(grep("pearson", colnames(results_li_rm)), grep("spearman", colnames(results_li_rm)))

correlations = cor(results_li_rm[,c(
  score_aggreg_order, 
  normalised_metrics[!(normalised_metrics %in% corr_metric)], 
  normalised_metrics[normalised_metrics %in% corr_metric], 
  unnormalised_metrics[!(unnormalised_metrics %in% corr_metric)],
  unnormalised_metrics[unnormalised_metrics %in% corr_metric]
)])
corrplot::corrplot(correlations, method='ellipse', type='full', outline=T, tl.col='black')
```

- Aitchison et pearson row (type cell) sont les plus différentiables. Le premiers est cependant du à des limites techniques (valeurs exponentielles lorsque proportions proches de 0).
- corrélations élevées -> metriques d'angles proche de RMSE et MAE

## Early_integration

```{r cor_dens_plot, fig.height=9}

# Fonction personnalisée pour corrélations avec couleurs et légende
cor_fun_colored <- function(data, mapping, method = "pearson", ndp = 2, sz = 4, ...) {
  x <- eval_data_col(data, mapping$x)
  y <- eval_data_col(data, mapping$y)
  corr <- cor(x, y, method = method, use = "complete.obs")
  
  # Palette de couleurs continue de -1 à 1
  # Utilisation d'une palette divergente : bleu (négatif) - blanc (0) - rouge (positif)
  color_palette <- colorRampPalette(c("#2166AC", "#F7F7F7", "#B2182B"))(100)
  color_index <- round((corr + 1) * 50) + 1  # Convertit [-1,1] en [1,100]
  color_index <- pmax(1, pmin(100, color_index))  # Assure que l'index est dans [1,100]
  col_cor <- color_palette[color_index]
  
  ggally_text(label = paste0("Corr ", method, " :\n", round(corr, ndp)), 
              color = col_cor, 
              size = sz,
              fontface = "bold") +
    theme_void()
}

density_fun_eimited <- function(data, mapping, ...) {
  ggplot(data = data, mapping = mapping) +
    geom_density2d(alpha = 0.6, color = "steelblue", ...) +
    xlim(0, 1) +
    ylim(0, 1)
}

# Version finale optimisée
ggpairs_final = ggpairs(
  results_ei,  # Dataset complet
  columns = c("score_aggreg", "rmse_norm", "pearson_col_norm", "spearman_col_norm", "pearson_row_norm", "spearman_row_norm", "aitchison_norm"),
  upper = list(continuous = cor_fun_colored),
  lower = list(continuous = density_fun_eimited),
  # lower = list(continuous = wrap("density", alpha = 0.6, bins = 10, fill = "steelblue", xeim = c(0,1), yeim = c(0,1))),
  diag = list(continuous = wrap("densityDiag", alpha = 0.8, fill = "lightblue"))
) + 
theme(
  strip.text = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
  axis.text = element_text(size = 8)
)

print(ggpairs_final)
```

- Corrélations élevées
- distribution marginales proches

```{r cor_plot, fig.height=9}
# results_ei[results_ei=="NaN"] = NA
row_rm = apply(results_ei,1,function(x) any(is.na(x)))
results_ei_rm = results_ei[!row_rm,-(1:(length(all_functions_ei)+2))]

score_aggreg_order = grep("score_aggreg", colnames(results_ei_rm))
normalised_metrics = grep("norm", colnames(results_ei_rm))
unnormalised_metrics = (1:ncol(results_ei_rm))[-c(score_aggreg_order, normalised_metrics)]
corr_metric = c(grep("pearson", colnames(results_ei_rm)), grep("spearman", colnames(results_ei_rm)))

correlations = cor(results_ei_rm[,c(
  score_aggreg_order, 
  normalised_metrics[!(normalised_metrics %in% corr_metric)], 
  normalised_metrics[normalised_metrics %in% corr_metric], 
  unnormalised_metrics[!(unnormalised_metrics %in% corr_metric)],
  unnormalised_metrics[unnormalised_metrics %in% corr_metric]
)])
corrplot::corrplot(correlations, method='ellipse', type='full', outline=T, tl.col='black')


# correlations = cor(rbind(
#   results_li_rm[,c(
#     score_aggreg_order, 
#     # normalised_metrics[!(normalised_metrics %in% corr_metric)], 
#     # normalised_metrics[normalised_metrics %in% corr_metric], 
#     unnormalised_metrics[!(unnormalised_metrics %in% corr_metric)],
#     unnormalised_metrics[unnormalised_metrics %in% corr_metric]
#   )],
#   results_ei_rm[,c(
#     score_aggreg_order, 
#     # normalised_metrics[!(normalised_metrics %in% corr_metric)], 
#     # normalised_metrics[normalised_metrics %in% corr_metric], 
#     unnormalised_metrics[!(unnormalised_metrics %in% corr_metric)],
#     unnormalised_metrics[unnormalised_metrics %in% corr_metric]
#   )]
# ))
# corrplot::corrplot(correlations, method='ellipse', type='full', outline=T, tl.col='black')
```

- Aitchison et pearson row (type cell) sont les plus différentiables. Le premier est cependant du à des limites techniques (valeurs exponentielles lorsque proportions proches de 0).
- corrélations élevées -> metriques d'angles proche de RMSE et MAE




# Florent stepwise model

## Late_integration

```{r stepwise_model}
foo = sapply(names(results_li), function(k) {
  print(k)
  main = k
  if (is.character(results_li[,k]) || is.factor(results_li[,k])) {
    barplot(table(results_li[,k], useNA="always"), las=2, main=main)
    return("char")
k  } else if (is.numeric(results_li[,k])){
      plot(density(results_li[,k], na.rm=TRUE), main=main)
      print(paste0("#NA: ", sum(is.na(results_li[,k]))))
    return("num")
  } else {
    stop(paste0("Data type not treated: ", k))
  }
})

bar = sapply(names(foo)[foo == "char"], function(k) {
  print(paste0(k, ": ", length(unique(results_li[,k]))))
  if (length(unique(results_li[,k]))>1) {
    return(k) 
  } else {
    return(NULL)
  }
})
facts = unlist(bar)
# score = "spearman_tot"
score = "score_aggreg"
f_lo = formula(paste0(score, "~1"))
f_up = formula(paste0(score, "~."))
m_lo = lm(f_lo, data=results_li[,c(facts, score)])
m_up = lm(f_up, data=results_li[,c(facts, score)])

any_line_na = sum(apply(results_li[,c(facts, score)], 1, \(ligne_i){any(is.na(ligne_i))}))
BIC_constant = log(nrow(results_li) - any_line_na)
life.lm = step(m_lo, method="forward", scope=list(upper=m_up,lower=m_lo), k = BIC_constant)
# Step:  AIC=-746072.7
# score_aggreg ~ deconvolution_rna + dataset + deconvolution_met + 
#     feature_selection_mixRNA + late_integration + preprocessing_mixRNA + 
#     preprocessing_mixMET + preprocessing_scRNA

life.lm = step(m_lo, method="both", scope=list(upper=m_up,lower=m_lo), k = BIC_constant)
# Step:  AIC=-746072.7
# score_aggreg ~ deconvolution_rna + dataset + deconvolution_met + 
#     feature_selection_mixRNA + late_integration + preprocessing_mixRNA + 
#     preprocessing_mixMET + preprocessing_scRNA


# spearman_tot ~ late_integration + deconvolution_rna + feature_selection_mixRNA + 
#     dataset + deconvolution_met + preprocessing_mixMET + preprocessing_mixRNA + 
#     feature_selection_mixMET


# boxplot(formula(paste0(score, "~late_integration")), results_li[,c(facts, score)])

# boxplot(formula(paste0(score, "~late_integration + deconvolution_rna")), results_li[,c(facts, score)], las=2)


boxplot(formula(paste0(score, "~dataset")), results_li[,c(facts, score)])

boxplot(formula(paste0(score, "~deconvolution_rna")), results_li[,c(facts, score)], las=2, na.rm = TRUE)

boxplot(formula(paste0(score, "~deconvolution_met")), results_li[,c(facts, score)], las=2, na.rm = TRUE)

```

## Early_integration

```{r stepwise_model}
foo = sapply(names(results_ei), function(k) {
  print(k)
  main = k
  if (is.character(results_ei[,k]) || is.factor(results_ei[,k])) {
    barplot(table(results_ei[,k], useNA="always"), las=2, main=main)
    return("char")
k  } else if (is.numeric(results_ei[,k])){
      plot(density(results_ei[,k], na.rm=TRUE), main=main)
      print(paste0("#NA: ", sum(is.na(results_ei[,k]))))
    return("num")
  } else {
    stop(paste0("Data type not treated: ", k))
  }
})

bar = sapply(names(foo)[foo == "char"], function(k) {
  print(paste0(k, ": ", length(unique(results_ei[,k]))))
  if (length(unique(results_ei[,k]))>1) {
    return(k) 
  } else {
    return(NULL)
  }
})
facts = unlist(bar)
# score = "spearman_tot"
score = "score_aggreg"
f_lo = formula(paste0(score, "~1"))
f_up = formula(paste0(score, "~."))
m_lo = lm(f_lo, data=results_ei[,c(facts, score)])
m_up = lm(f_up, data=results_ei[,c(facts, score)])

any_eine_na = sum(apply(results_ei[,c(facts, score)], 1, \(eigne_i){any(is.na(eigne_i))}))
BIC_constant = log(nrow(results_ei) - any_eine_na)
eife.lm = step(m_lo, method="forward", scope=list(upper=m_up,lower=m_lo), k = BIC_constant)
# Step:  AIC=-949041.8
# score_aggreg ~ early_integration + dataset + deconvolution + 
#     preprocessing_mixRNA + feature_selection_mixRNA + preprocessing_mixMET + 
#     feature_selection_mixMET

eife.lm = step(m_lo, method="both", scope=list(upper=m_up,lower=m_lo), k = BIC_constant)
# Step:  AIC=-949041.8
# score_aggreg ~ early_integration + dataset + deconvolution + 
#     preprocessing_mixRNA + feature_selection_mixRNA + preprocessing_mixMET + 
#     feature_selection_mixMET


# spearman_tot ~ early_integration + deconvolution_rna + feature_selection_mixRNA + 
#     dataset + deconvolution_met + preprocessing_mixMET + preprocessing_mixRNA + 
#     feature_selection_mixMET


# boxplot(formula(paste0(score, "~early_integration")), results_ei[,c(facts, score)])

# boxplot(formula(paste0(score, "~early_integration + deconvolution_rna")), results_ei[,c(facts, score)], las=2)


boxplot(formula(paste0(score, "~dataset")), results_ei[,c(facts, score)])

boxplot(formula(paste0(score, "~deconvolution")), results_ei[,c(facts, score)], las=2, na.rm = TRUE)

```


# PCA

## Late_integration

```{r val_propre_pca_standardise}
results_li_stand = results_li_all_dataset %>% # median isn't allowed for PCA
  group_by(dataset) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ .x - mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
  ungroup() %>% 
  as.data.frame()

# We remove the in vivo dataset
results_li_stand_rm = results_li_stand[results_li_stand$dataset != "invivo1", ] # -c(which(colnames(results_li_stand) %in% c("jsd", "jsd_norm")))


score_aggreg_order = grep("score_aggreg", colnames(results_li_stand_rm))
normalised_metrics = grep("norm", colnames(results_li_stand_rm))
unnormalised_metrics = (1:ncol(results_li_stand_rm))[-c(1:(length(all_functions_li)+2), score_aggreg_order, normalised_metrics)]
corr_metric = c(grep("pearson", colnames(results_li_stand_rm)), grep("spearman", colnames(results_li_stand_rm)))
stepwise_most_var = which(colnames(results_li_stand_rm) %in% c("late_integration", "deconvolution_rna", "deconvolution_met"))

pca_results_li_stand_rm = PCA(results_li_stand_rm[,c(score_aggreg_order, stepwise_most_var, unnormalised_metrics)], quanti.sup = 1, quali.sup = 2:(length(stepwise_most_var)+1), graph = FALSE)
barplot(pca_results_li_stand_rm$eig[,2])
```

Les trois premières composantes seront analysées, représentant  toujours 96% de la variabilité totale des différentes métriques. La différence est très minime entre données de base et celle standardisé par dataset, la proportion de varance expliquée par la première dimension est plus élevé dans le second cas alors que celles de la deuxième et troisième plus faible.


```{r var_plot_pca_standardise}
# par(mfrow = c(3,2))
# plot(pca_results_li_stand_rm, axes = c(1, 2), choix="ind")
plot(pca_results_li_stand_rm, axes = c(1, 2), choix="var")
# plot(pca_results_li_stand_rm, axes = c(1, 3), choix="ind")
plot(pca_results_li_stand_rm, axes = c(1, 3), choix="var")
# plot(pca_results_li_stand_rm, axes = c(2, 3), choix="ind")
plot(pca_results_li_stand_rm, axes = c(2, 3), choix="var")
# par(mfrow = c(1,1))
```

```{r dim_desc_pca_standardise}
dimdesc(pca_results_li_stand_rm)
```

On a des axes bien plus séparé 
- Premier axe construit par l'ensemble des variables (moins aitchison)
- second axe construit uniquement par les corrélations en lignes (types cell)
- troisième axe construit uniquement par aitchison
On voit également que le score aggreg est quasiment parfaitement corrélé au premier axes de cette PCA.


```{r new_plot_ellipses_standardise}
plot_density_groups = function(res_pca, axes = c(1,2), alpha = 0.7) {
  
  # Extraction des coordonnées individuelles
  if("PCA" %in% class(res_pca)) {
    coord_ind = res_pca$ind$coord[, axes]
    quali_sup = res_pca$call$quali.sup
  } else if("CA" %in% class(res_pca)) {
    coord_ind = res_pca$row$coord[, axes]
    quali_sup = res_pca$call$quali.sup  
  } else if("MCA" %in% class(res_pca)) {
    coord_ind = res_pca$ind$coord[, axes]
    quali_sup = res_pca$call$quali.sup
  }
  
  # Récupération des données originales
  data_orig = res_pca$call$X
  
  library(ggplot2)
  library(ggrepel)
  
  # Liste pour stocker les graphiques
  plots_list = list()
  
  # Boucle sur chaque variable qualitative supplémentaire
  for(i in 1:length(quali_sup$numero)) {
    var_name = colnames(quali_sup$quali.sup)[i]
    quali_var = data_orig[, quali_sup$numero[i]]
    
    # Dataframe pour le graphique
    df = data.frame(
      Dim1 = coord_ind[, 1],
      Dim2 = coord_ind[, 2],
      groupe = quali_var
    )
    
    # Calcul des barycentres
    barycentres = aggregate(cbind(Dim1, Dim2) ~ groupe, df, mean)
    
    # Création du graphique
    p = ggplot(df, aes(x = Dim1, y = Dim2, color = groupe)) +
      geom_density_2d(alpha = alpha) +
      geom_point(data = barycentres, size = 3) +
      geom_text_repel(data = barycentres, aes(label = groupe)) +
      labs(
        title = var_name, color = var_name, 
        x = paste0("Dim", axes[1]), y = paste0("Dim", axes[2])
      ) +
      theme(legend.position = "bottom")
    
    plots_list[[var_name]] = p
  }
  
  # Affichage du panel
  if(length(plots_list) > 1) {
    gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = plots_list, ncol = 2)
  } else {
    print(plots_list[[1]])
  }
}

plot_density_groups(res_pca = pca_results_li_stand_rm, axes = c(1,2))
plot_density_groups(res_pca = pca_results_li_stand_rm, axes = c(1,3))
plot_density_groups(res_pca = pca_results_li_stand_rm, axes = c(2,3))
```

Selon le graphique des axes 1 et 2, On retrouve des conclusions que l'on a déjà pu avoir : 
- pour la deconvolution RNA, c'est RLR, RLRpoisson et node (donc onlyMET) qui ont les valeurs moyennes les plus grandes, donc les meilleurs resultats selon le score aggreg puisqu'il est fortement corrélé à cette axe.
- pour la deconvolution RNA, on voit bien que node (donc Only RNA) a une valeur faible pour ce premier axe, donc n'a pas de bon résultat pour le score aggreg. On retrouve ces conclusions dans le graphique de late Integration

Pour les dimensions 2 et 3, cela nous donne de nouveau indices concernant certaine méthode :
- Les méthodes RLR et RLRpoisson ont tendances à plus données des estimation à 0 que lm ou nnls
- On voit un différence sur la deuxième dimension entre RLR et RLRpoisson, donc la seconde méthode donne des estimations avec un ordre des types cellulaires globalement plus corrélée que la première 
- La meilleur méthode d'intégration tardive liMeanRMSE a un coefficient faible pour la seconde dimension de l'ACP, donc produit de bonne estimation globale des proportions des types cellulaires mais ne donne pas le bonne ordre de ceux-ci (puisque les corrélation vont de -1 à 1). Paradoxalement, OnlyRNA ne donne pas de bonne estimatio mais le bonne ordre.
- OnlyRNA est la méthode d'integration tardives a une très forte valeur pour la troisième dimension (cette modalité doit construire à elle seul cet axe), donc OnlyRNA (soit les méthodes de déconvolution sur le transcriptome) a tendance à produire beaucoup d'estimation à 0, puisque c'est cela qui fait explosé la distance d'Aitchison qui est la seul métrique fortement corrélé à cet axe. Cependant, OnlyMET est la seconde valeur la plus forte donc on pourrait donner la même conclusions dans une moindre mesure.

## Early_integration

```{r val_propre_pca_standardise}
results_ei_stand <- results_ei_all_dataset %>%
  group_by(dataset) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ .x - mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
  ungroup() %>% 
  as.data.frame()

# Then for the PCA, we remove jsd (which is highly correlated to other metrics) and the in vivo dataset
results_ei_stand_rm = results_ei_stand[results_ei_stand$dataset != "invivo1",] #  -c(which(colnames(results_ei_stand) %in% c("jsd", "jsd_norm")))


score_aggreg_order = grep("score_aggreg", colnames(results_ei_stand_rm))
normalised_metrics = grep("norm", colnames(results_ei_stand_rm))
unnormalised_metrics = (1:ncol(results_ei_stand_rm))[-c(1:(length(all_functions_ei)+2), score_aggreg_order, normalised_metrics)]
corr_metric = c(grep("pearson", colnames(results_ei_stand_rm)), grep("spearman", colnames(results_ei_stand_rm)))
stepwise_most_var = which(colnames(results_ei_stand_rm) %in% c("early_integration", "deconvolution_rna", "deconvolution_met"))

pca_results_ei_stand_rm = PCA(results_ei_stand_rm[,c(score_aggreg_order, stepwise_most_var, unnormalised_metrics)], quanti.sup = 1, quali.sup = 2:(length(stepwise_most_var)+1), graph = FALSE)
barplot(pca_results_ei_stand_rm$eig[,2])
```

Les trois premières composantes seront analysées, représentant  toujours 94% de la variabieité totale des différentes métriques. La différence est très minime entre données de base et celle standardisé par dataset, la proportion de varance expeiquée par la première dimension est plus élevé dans le second cas alors que celles de la deuxième et troisième plus faible.


```{r var_plot_pca_standardise}
# par(mfrow = c(3,2))
# plot(pca_results_ei_stand_rm, axes = c(1, 2), choix="ind")
plot(pca_results_ei_stand_rm, axes = c(1, 2), choix="var")
# plot(pca_results_ei_stand_rm, axes = c(1, 3), choix="ind")
plot(pca_results_ei_stand_rm, axes = c(1, 3), choix="var")
# plot(pca_results_ei_stand_rm, axes = c(2, 3), choix="ind")
plot(pca_results_ei_stand_rm, axes = c(2, 3), choix="var")
# par(mfrow = c(1,1))
```

```{r dim_desc_pca_standardise}
dimdesc(pca_results_ei_stand_rm)
```

On a des axes bien plus séparé 
- Premier axe construit par l'ensemble des variables (moins aitcison)
- second axe construit uniquement par les corrélations en eignes (types cell)
- troisième axe construit uniquement par aitchison
On voit également que le score aggreg est quasiment parfaitement corrélé au premier axes de cette PCA.


```{r new_plot_eleipses_standardise}
plot_density_groups = function(res_pca, axes = c(1,2), alpha = 0.7) {
  
  # Extraction des coordonnées individuelles
  if("PCA" %in% class(res_pca)) {
    coord_ind = res_pca$ind$coord[, axes]
    quali_sup = res_pca$call$quali.sup
  } else if("CA" %in% class(res_pca)) {
    coord_ind = res_pca$row$coord[, axes]
    quali_sup = res_pca$call$quali.sup  
  } else if("MCA" %in% class(res_pca)) {
    coord_ind = res_pca$ind$coord[, axes]
    quali_sup = res_pca$call$quali.sup
  }
  
  # Récupération des données originales
  data_orig = res_pca$call$X
  
  library(ggplot2)
  library(ggrepel)
  
  # Liste pour stocker les graphiques
  plots_list = list()
  
  # Boucle sur chaque variable qualitative supplémentaire
  for(i in 1:length(quali_sup$numero)) {
    var_name = colnames(quali_sup$quali.sup)[i]
    quali_var = data_orig[, quali_sup$numero[i]]
    
    # Dataframe pour le graphique
    df = data.frame(
      Dim1 = coord_ind[, 1],
      Dim2 = coord_ind[, 2],
      groupe = quali_var
    )
    
    # Calcul des barycentres
    barycentres = aggregate(cbind(Dim1, Dim2) ~ groupe, df, mean)
    
    # Création du graphique
    p = ggplot(df, aes(x = Dim1, y = Dim2, color = groupe)) +
      geom_density_2d(alpha = alpha) +
      geom_point(data = barycentres, size = 3) +
      geom_text_repel(data = barycentres, aes(label = groupe)) +
      labs(
        title = var_name, color = var_name, 
        x = paste0("Dim", axes[1]), y = paste0("Dim", axes[2])
      ) +
      theme(legend.position = "bottom")
    
    plots_list[[var_name]] = p
  }
  
  # Affichage du panel
  if(length(plots_list) > 1) {
    gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = plots_list, ncol = 2)
  } else {
    print(plots_list[[1]])
  }
}

plot_density_groups(res_pca = pca_results_ei_stand_rm, axes = c(1,2))
plot_density_groups(res_pca = pca_results_ei_stand_rm, axes = c(1,3))
plot_density_groups(res_pca = pca_results_ei_stand_rm, axes = c(2,3))
```

Selon le graphique des axes 1 et 2, On retrouve des conclusions que l'on a déjà pu avoir : 
- pour la deconvolution RNA, c'est RLR, RLRpoisson et node (donc onlyMET) qui ont les valeurs moyennes les plus grandes, donc les meilleurs resultats selon le score aggreg puisqu'il est fortement corrélé à cette axe.
- pour la deconvolution RNA, on voit bien que node (donc Only RNA) a une valeur faible pour ce premier axe, donc n'a pas de bon résultat pour le score aggreg. On retrouve ces conclusions dans le graphique de late Integration

Pour les dimensions 2 et 3, cela nous donne de nouveau indices concernant certaine méthode :
- Les méthodes RLR et RLRpoisson ont tendances à plus données des estimation à 0 que lm ou nnls
- On voit un différence sur la deuxième dimension entre RLR et RLRpoisson, donc la seconde méthode donne des estimations avec un ordre des types cellulaires globalement plus corrélée que la première 
- La meilleur méthode d'intégration tardive eiMeanRMSE a un coefficient faible pour la seconde dimension de l'ACP, donc produit de bonne estimation globale des proportions des types cellulaires mais ne donne pas le bonne ordre de ceux-ci (puisque les corrélation vont de -1 à 1). Paradoxalement, OnlyRNA ne donne pas de bonne estimatio mais le bonne ordre.
- OnlyRNA est la méthode d'integration tardives a une très forte valeur pour la troisième dimension (cette modaeité doit construire à elle seul cet axe), donc OnlyRNA (soit les méthodes de déconvolution sur le transcriptome) a tendance à produire beaucoup d'estimation à 0, puisque c'est cela qui fait explosé la distance d'Aitchison qui est la seul métrique fortement corrélé à cet axe. Cependant, OnlyMET est la seconde valeur la plus forte donc on pourrait donner la même conclusions dans une moindre mesure.
